text:
One of the factors contributing to the recent Source Evaluation Form petition is the perceived need for an impact factor for Polish journals in the humanities and social sciences (HSS). An adequate response to this requirement demands more than a simple assertion that the current situation is satisfactory, and accordingly we describe here the creation and content of POL-index, a national citation database of Polish journals in HSS. The need for such a database arose because, unlike many countries whose journals are systematically covered by SCOPUS and/or the Web of Science, in Poland there has been no journal-specific impact factor to apply to HSS journal evaluations. The POL-index database overcomes field-specific problems arising from, for example, longer citation half-lives and non-standard citation distributions through use of a multifaceted data collection procedure that incorporates not only manual entries from journal publishers but also selective automated XML journal harvesting from SCOPUS.

The core of the system is Polish Impact Factor (PWW) calculated in such way to guarantee stability of the results in time and consistency with respect to the research field. The applied formula responds to the specificity of citation practices in the field of Humanities and Social Sciences at the same time respecting requirements stemming from rapid changes in citation data in time, what is achieved through calculation of citations given within five last years with the dominating share of citations in the two latest years. We present here a technical part of the core system – citation recognition module and verification blocks and also show here the general idea of plans concerning further development of the system from a level of fully manual input of required data to automatic working including also monographs and other types of publications. The POL-index is intended for providing an unquestionable evidences for Minister’s assessment of the position of Polish scholarly journals.
headline: Fully Human Written
prediction: We believe that this document is fully human-written
